skills: ambiguous-image holiday interpretation (generic "Happy Holidays" is sometimes the correct answer when the image doesn't specify one holiday)
dialogue paraphrase-matching (casual chat -> formal restatement of transport mode/duration)
culture-specific picture classification (identify "American sport" among sumo/volleyball/boxing/American football)
fact-restatement with negation logic (platypus has no stomach -> correct negated paraphrase, distractor over-generalises to "every animal")
family/entity counting logic from named references (avoid double-counting, avoid wrong groupings)
notice title-selection (best name/heading for a short rules list)
sign-to-scenario matching (choosing which of several similar prohibition icons matches a stated scenario)
menu-based arithmetic (drink price table + order -> total cost)
complex multi-series bar-chart selection matching a comparative statement across multiple categories and two years
multi-turn map/direction reading with named roads
word-order sentence correctness (compound subject + adverbial phrase placement)
advanced punctuation correctness (comma/semicolon/colon nuance, not just question marks)
guess-and-correct dialogue (wrong guess about an event/holiday is corrected by the picture)
dialogue completion across many everyday settings (18 items, items 15-32)
grammar: word order with compound subjects and adverbials (My friends and I go to the museum once a week)
modal verbs and requests (Could you help me)
punctuation: comma before question tags, colon/semicolon misuse
question_types: family-counting logic from a short passage
short 2-line dialogue completion across many settings (18 items)
reading_patterns: short descriptive passages that must be restated correctly with attention to negation and quantity words (no stomach vs one stomach; three siblings named vs a wrong total)
dialogue_patterns: casual-chat paraphrase exchange (trip details restated in a different sentence structure)
opinion/agreement-disagreement exchange (colour choice for a project)
future-plan exchange matched topically to a stated hobby
distractor_patterns: over-generalised negation distractor (every animal does not have a stomach)
wrong-total or wrong-grouping distractor in family/entity counting
near-identical prohibition icon distractor (no food vs no drink)
subtle numeric variation between otherwise-identical bar charts
wrong punctuation mark for the sentence type (semicolon/exclamation where a comma or period is needed)
mismatched register or topic in dialogue replies
answer_logic: when an image is genuinely ambiguous about which specific holiday it depicts, the generic option is the intended correct answer
fact/negation passages require precise negation-matching, not just topical relevance
family/entity-counting items require tallying only the specific named individuals, not implied or default groupings
multi-series chart items require checking every category across both years/comparisons, not just the most visually obvious bar
notes: Exam sat 11 Feb 2566 (BE+1 sitting-date convention). 32 items; items 1-14 standalone/reading, items 15-32 dialogue completion. Punctuation and word-order items grow more sophisticated than earlier years (colon/semicolon nuance, compound-subject word order).
